name: workplace_health_near_miss_event_kestrel_auto_ben
premise: A Workplace Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving workplace health near miss event kestrel auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Darius Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Darius Ishikawa, accountable for a defensible call in Workplace Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Darius Bennett
agents.identity: I am Darius Bennett, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Workplace Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Darius Ishikawa must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Darius Ishikawa do?
